behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: This checks cross-module handoff (file load + env overlay + schema validation + error formatting). Unit tests on each piece can miss a wiring bug where the missing-path reported is wrong after merge. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require subprocess/real HTTP/external contract, only in-process config loading and message content. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI model is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader against a temp config file missing `database.host` with `TOOL_` env cleared, then assert the raised startup error includes exact path `database.host` from the integrated load/merge/validate path.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when env resolution logic and path-construction logic interact; unit tests of path helpers alone can give false confidence. Rejected System because behavior text only requires local path resolution semantics, not subprocess behavior or external API/contract checks. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory, place config there, invoke loader in-process, and assert it reads from `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tool/config.yaml` instead of `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: This is precedence behavior across two config sources and merge order, a classic multi-module interaction risk. Unit tests on parser/mapper alone can miss source-priority bugs. Rejected System because no explicit external contract or subprocess/network requirement is stated. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness is deterministic and non-LLM.
behaviors.test_description: Create file value for a key and set conflicting `TOOL_...` env var, run loader through full merge path, and assert resulting effective config uses env value.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Needs real interaction between YAML parser exception payload and CLI error rendering; unit tests of formatter alone can miss dropped line numbers. Rejected System because behavior text does not require subprocess round-trip or external service contract, only local parse/error propagation. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: Provide malformed YAML in temp config file, invoke loader, and assert startup error includes parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires an externally visible filesystem side effect and readback (directory + file creation) at startup, which triggers SYSTEM TRIAD #3 (round-trip side effect visibility). Mock filesystem can hide real OS path/permission/timing issues; real FS observation is required to avoid false confidence. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run CLI (or startup loader path) with a real temp HOME/XDG location where config dir is absent, then verify on disk that directory and defaults file were created and startup proceeds successfully.
